Re: Your Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Weems:
You have requested advice on behalf of Assemblyman Tom

Reform Act. 1/

May Assembtyman Mcclintock contribute funds frorn his
federal co.mitte" to tris state Assembly conrnittee?
2. 
May Assemblyman Mcclintock contribute funds from his
state Assembly cornmittee to his federal committee?

Federal candidates' committees are not rrrecipient committees'r
under California law. (Stark Advice Letter, No. A-89-47o, and
Marsh Advice Letter, No. I-89-056, copies enclosed.) Such
cornrnittees are considered rrpersonstt for purposes of deternining
their contribution Iinits under Proposition 73. However, on
September 25, L99o, the United States District court for the
Eastern District of California invalidated the state's fiscal year
contribution limits, which were added to the Act by passage of
Proposition 73 in 1988. (Service Emplovees International Union,
AFL― CIO. CLC, et at. v. Fair Political Practices Commission (■
990)
747 F. Supp. 580. (1=SEIUl=)。
)
Therefore, Assemblyman
Mcclintock's federal committee nay make contributions to his
Assembly comrnittee. Such contributions are not Iimited unless the
assembl| conmittee is estabLished for a special election.3/ 
If
the federal committee makes contributions in the amount of $1,0,00o
or more during a calendar year, the federal comrnittee wi1l, qualify
as a major donor comrnittee and wilL have reporting requirements
under the Act. 
(Section 82013.) Please refer to the FPPC
Infornation ManuaI E on campaiqn Disclosure Provisions of the
Political 
Reform Act (copy enclosed) for information concerning
discfosure by major donor conmittees.
Effective January 1, 1990, the Act was arnended to include
provisions that regulate the appropriate use of campaign funds.
(Section 895L1, et seCI. the 'rpersonal userr Iaw. ) Section 89512
states in part that ca:npaign funds may be expended if such
expenditure is reasonably related to a political 
purpose. whereas
a transfer of contributions from Assembl)rman McClintock's state
Assembly cornmittee to his federal committee would appear to meet
the "political 
purposerr test in Section 895]-2t Section 89510
imposes a further requirement that the funds be held in trust for
expenses associated with his election to the state Assembly.
However, in SEIU the court ruled that restrictions on transfers of
contributions between a candidate,s own comnittees violated the
candj.date's First Arnendrnent rights. 
AccordingLy, untess the
district 
court,s decision in SEIU is reversed by the appell,ate
court, we are advising that contributions from Assemblyman
McClintock's state Assembly conmittee to his federal committee are
pernissible.
2/ 
The decision in SEIU has been appealed. our advice is
subject to change depending upon the outcome of this appeal.
3/ 
Please note that contribution linits 
for state and Local
special elections and special runoff elections were not affected
by the courf's decision and are still 
in effect.


Weems, John
July 31, 19 91
Page 3
In addition, you nay wish to contact the Federal Elections
Commission with respect to any relevant federal- laws regarding
permissible uses of funds held by a federal candidate,s committee,
and transfer of funds to the federal committee.
Please do not hesitate to contact ne at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.
S incere ly ,
Scott Hallabrin

By:- Lynda Cassady

sections.question: 1. May Assemblyman Mcclintock contribute funds from his federal committee to his state Assembly committee? 2. May Assemblyman Mcclintock contribute funds from his state Assembly committee to his federal committee?
sections.conclusion: 1. Assemblyman McClinotck may contribute funds from his federal committee to his state Assembly committee. 2. Assemblyman Mcclintock may contribute funds from his state Assembly committee to his federal committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Assemblyman McClintock may transfer campaign funds between his federal committee and state Assembly committee in either direction.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both transfers are permissible: federal committee to state Assembly committee contributions are allowed without limits (unless $10,000+ triggers major donor reporting), and state Assembly committee to federal committee contributions are permissible based on SEIU court decision invalidating transfer restrictions as First Amendment violations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with detailed ANALYSIS. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'raccf intoct' instead of 'McClintock'). File number A-91-326 indicates this is an official FPPC advice letter. Advice conditional on appeal outcome of SEIU case.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. May Assemblyman Mcclintock contribute funds from his federal committee to his state Assembly committee? 2. May Assemblyman Mcclintock contribute funds from his state Assembly committee to his federal committee?

CONCLUSION: 1. Assemblyman McClinotck may contribute funds from his federal committee to his state Assembly committee. 2. Assemblyman Mcclintock may contribute funds from his state Assembly committee to his federal committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Assemblyman McClintock that he may transfer campaign funds in both directions between his federal and state Assembly committees, citing the SEIU court decision invalidating contribution transfer restrictions as First Amendment violations.
